programme.desc: An investigation into the rights and wrongs of male circumcision, one of the most common surgical procedures in the world. programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the development progress of properties in Canterbury in Kent, London's Crystal Palace and the Cumbria village of Branthwaite(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Jayne Myburgh thought her son Albie's rare genetic disorder would be greater controlled in the warmth of the Australian sun. Her South African-born husband Eugene was well aware of his wife's dreams, but needed more than a promise of sunshine to convince him he should move to the other side of the world. Nicki Chapman finds out whether or not they made the life-changing decision to relocate Down Under(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers investigate the death of a car wash worker, and catch two crooks dealing in counterfeit medicines worth £5million(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Agricultural Showground on Anglesey, where antique experts Roo Irvine and Richard Madley guide two teams as they search for items of interest and value to take to auction. Away from the contest, Charlie also meets a familiar face with a passion for Georgian glass(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Zara goes into battle for Daniel's job at the prison and Emma is surprised by the strength of her feelings for Jasmine. Tragedy strikes for Al and a homeless mother, while Karen and Rob start a new fostering chapter(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu must track down a vital witness in a crown court trial. Mark Benton and Jo Joyner star in the comedy drama about an oddball couple of private detectives investigating criminal acts in Stratford-upon-Avon(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple with a budget of £300,00 find a country property that gives them community on their doorstep and a crash pad for their grandchildren to stay during the holidays. Margherita also meets a Devon stovemaker and finds out the practical and environmental benefits of this popular country home interior feature(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Three influential farm workers - all critical to the success of their farms - go head to head, hoping to be named Farm Worker of the Year at the prestigious Farmers Weekly Awards(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Margie Cooper and Charles Hanson reach the final leg of their trip and steer their classic car through Yorkshire. Charles decides to spend as much of his kitty as possible, hoping a bigger outlay will result in huge profits. He goes for age, finding a pair of expensive 17th-century oak chairs. Margie also goes 17th century, but finds a crib that's much more within budget. The final auction is in Harrogate, North Yorkshire(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rob Beckett present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers compete to design a garden for a family of seven in Stamford, Lincolnshire, who have just moved from rural Rutland and miss the countryside. With five noisy children, the parents want a peaceful garden that they can call their own, but the awkward plot gives the presenters a huge challenge, despite a budget of £4,500(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Crystal Palace left-back Patrick van Aanholt, former Premier League star Jimmy Floyd Hasselbaink, England cricketer Samit Patel and tennis player Naomi Broady join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chaos and panic ensues as the party goers struggle to comprehend what has happened. As the grave situation grows worse, multiple lives are left hanging in the balance and one person is set to meet a tragic end(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for another episode of previously unseen material from the hit comedy panel show. Among the participants setting out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves are Clare Balding, Liz Bonnin, Jo Brand, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Greg Davies, Simon Day, Greg James, Guz Khan, Gabby Logan, Chris McCausland, Stephen Merchant, Kiri Pritchard-McLean, Angela Scanlon, John Simm, Ranj Singh, Henning Wehn, Claudia Winkleman and Lucy Worsley(n)

programme.title: Bumps
programme.desc: Sixty-two-year-old divorcee Anita is concerned that despite having two grown up kids, there is no stork on the horizon to bring her grandchildren. Her daughter Joanne cannot have children and her son Aiden has zero interest. After hearing about an Indian woman in her seventies giving birth, Anita secretly undergoes treatment - only to learn Joanne has also managed to conceive and is due at the same time. One-off comedy, starring Amanda Redman, with Lisa McGrillis and Seb Cardinal(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham brings the 26th series of his hit chat show to an end by dipping back into its archives to recall some of the more memorable verbal exchanges. Stars appearing include Robert De Niro, Bruce Springsteen, Arnold Schwarzenegger, Nadiya Hussain, Emilia Clarke, Regina King, Julie Andrews, Jennifer Aniston, Reese Witherspoon, Olivia Colman, Helena Bonham Carter, Elizabeth Banks, Ricky Gervais, Lewis Hamilton, Hillary Clinton, Dwayne Johnson, Kevin Hart, Jodie Whittaker, Judi Dench, Jennifer Hudson, Hugh Grant, Daisy Ridley, Ruth Jones, Motsi Mabuse, Anthony Joshua and Tom Hanks(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: Tensions are high and emotions are charged as the remaining four queens are challenged to give family members a very special drag makeover. Bafta winner Michaela Coel joins Michelle Visage and Alan Carr on the judging panel as another queen is eliminated from the competition(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with the Donnelly family who spent a trial week in Melbourne in 2018, and discovers which country they have since chosen to live in(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the City Hall in Kingston-upon-Hull, antiques experts Caroline Hawley and David Harper hunt for objects to take to auction. Caroline is entertained by the story behind some Laurel and Hardy autographs and David is gripped by a collection of postcards between young lovers from the Edwardian era. Presenter Paul Martin talks to playwright James Graham about the legacy of Hull's year as the UK's City of Culture(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Dundee to Aberdeen, stopping off at Glamis Castle, where the Queen Mother grew up, and making an excursion into the Eastern Highlands(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar goes on a ramble that straddles the north and south sides of Calderdale, stopping along the way to sketch and read poetry. She begins in the village of Heptonstall, rambles past Hell Hole and along the canal in Hebden Bridge, before climbing to the Stoodley Pike peace monument(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Walks of Life
programme.desc: JB Gill searches for hidden gems amongst Edinburgh's more familiar tourist sites, and discovers why one of the city's most important religious figures was buried in a car park(n)

programme.title: World's Sneakiest Animals
programme.desc: Naturalist Chris Packham observes the devious tactics and sneaky tricks that some animals carry out in order to survive. New, ground-breaking science helps reveal astonishing visual trickery, from illusionist zebras to vanishing geckos and shape-shifting cuttlefish. Mischievous mimics, from snakes to baby birds disguised as caterpillars, and a lioness who looks like a male lion are also highlighted. The world's ultimate con artists can even rely on sneaky strategies against their own kind, such as the monkey that cheats its family and the lizard that fakes its death(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: A sudden showdown ends in death, after whispered last words. Patricia is prompted to come clean to Johno, and it remains to be seen whether Keith can escape the crime scene(n)

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians reveal how the railways transformed the British diet, rescuing a nation that was struggling to feed itself. Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn discover how the mass transportation of livestock gave birth to the traditional British roast, and Ruth Goodman is in Whitby, which became a thriving fishing town thanks to the North Yorkshire Moors Railway supplying its kippers. Plus, Hampshire's watercress line, which brought nutritional salad to the masses, and how the use of steam-powered engines revolutionised production at Britain's oldest brewery(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet UK
programme.desc: Steve Brown makes a jaw-dropping discovery of sharks' teeth on the beach. Plus, Chris Packham meets a photographer who believes the UK has the best marine life in the world(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Revisiting historic venues from the series, alongside new valuations and unexpected auctions. Paul Martin visits the Cotswolds village of Broadway to uncover its artistic past(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jamelia, Susan Calman, Gareth Malone and Paul Martin test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Melaka, for centuries one of the greatest trading ports in the world, where he tastes the spicy cuisine of the Kristang community. Continuing to Kluang amid fertile farmland, Michael joins in the pineapple harvest and discovers how Malaysia's resources of tin and tropical fruit created a global market in food. Finally, he arrives at the tip of the Malaysian peninsula in Johor Bahru(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Olympic gold medal-winning swimmer Rebecca Adlington and TV presenter Helen Skelton rummage for items of interest - and value - in the north west of England, assisted by antique experts Roo Irvine and Tim Medhurst. En route to auction in Bolton, they discover the salty secret of Cheshire cheese and hear the story of how, thanks to a Hawaiian Duke, swimming was changed for ever(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: In the final episode, the chef and the group of volunteers discover how much weight they have lost over the past two months, and how much fitter they are as a result of all their hard work. To celebrate their achievements, Tom decides to throw a special picnic by the river, and whips up a series of exciting, healthy recipes they can all enjoy(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: In the final episode, Michael Mosley investigates whether simply thinking about food can help you lose weight, and Giles Yeo finds out how to keep your skin young and healthy. Meanwhile, Alain Gregoire tests whether listening to music improves your sleep, and Guddi Singh examines the latest diet in the headlines which claims to have a good impact on the planet(n)

programme.title: Good Omens
programme.desc: As the series reaches its dramatic conclusion, Adam, Crowley and Aziraphale must work together to save the world from certain doom. Will Heaven and Hell punish Crowley and Aziraphale for betraying them if they do? Dramatisation of Terry Pratchett and Neil Gaiman's book, starring Michael Sheen and David Tennant(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Award-winning stand-up comedian Dane Baptiste plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience and then introduces routines by New York comic Desiree Burch and Liverpool funnyman Chris McCausland(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Universal Credit: Inside the Welfare State
programme.desc: The final episode of the series comes from Bolton, a city with unusually high levels of unemployment for people between 18 and 25. Waitress Jenny works on a zero hour contract, and since universal credit is paid a month in arrears, she finds it very difficult to predict how much money she will have to live off at any given time. New claimant Paula is given an advance payment to ease her through the five-week transition period to universal credit, but finds herself struggling to pay it back and live on her monthly payments(n)

programme.title: Travels in Euroland with Ed Balls
programme.desc: The former MP travels to Italy and France, finding out how years of austerity have affected politics across Europe. In Italy, he compares the lives of multimillionaire fashion entrepreneur Valeria Marini, who lives in Italy's wealthiest city, Milan, and a farming family from Calabria, one of the poorest regions. Ed ends his trip in France, where Marine le Pen has increased the vote for her rebranded National Rally party, in part by promising to protect French industry from foreign traders. He meets voters unsure which way to turn and asks what the future holds for European politics(n)

programme.title: Earth's Tropical Islands
programme.desc: A look across Hawaii's varied islands, discovering how they were made, and how its wildlife thrives. The most remote island chain on Earth, its tropical shores are hard to reach. However, for the hardy creatures that have made it there, such as the waterfall-climbing fish, carnivorous caterpillar and Laysan albatross, a land of opportunity awaits. From newly formed lava fields, to lush jungles, and vibrant coral reefs, these diverse and beautiful islands have it all. As this documentary reveals, its people are looking to the future and attempting to protect and foster its flora and fauna(n)

programme.title: Mary Beard's Shock of the Nude
programme.desc: Part one of two. The classicist examines the nude in western art and its troubling power to provoke ideas about gender, sex and moral transgression. Mary starts with a statue of Aphrodite by Praxiteles and Titian's Venus of Urbino, asking how a woman like her should respond to the artworks, and considers the challenge of depicting the female nude for a woman artist by looking at the work of 17th-century painter Artemisia Gentileschi. In a life-drawing class, Mary joins a hen party as they attempt to sketch a naked man(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with the Bone family, who spent a trial week sampling life in Australia, to see if they decided to make a permanent move down under or stay put in Scotland(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp hosts from Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, where experts Thomas Forrester and Tim Weeks help competing teams bag the best buys to make a profit at auction. Natasha also investigates a mystery item that dates back to the mid-19th century(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is at Elgin's port, the coastal town of Lossiemouth, where the Labour Party's first prime minister James Ramsay MacDonald was born. Nearby, Michael finds a remote boarding school established in the 1930s and famous today both for its unusual ideas and its royal former pupils. His penultimate stop is Inverness, where he uncovers the work of female photographer MEM Donaldson, who documented a Highland way of life that was rapidly disappearing. Michael's journey ends at Loch Ness, where he joins a Deep Scan research team scouring the deep for signs of the elusive monster(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: The volunteers are short on time for cooking and exercise, so Tom offers solutions to keep them on track, including quick and delicious recipes, and tips for planning ahead. For the first recipe, Tom makes a pot of beef mince - the starting point for some of the nation's favourite dishes. He uses the batch to make a cottage pie, while the rest is frozen to be used later, perhaps for chilli con carne, spaghetti bolognese or lasagne(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Walks of Life
programme.desc: Mehreen Baig follows an ancient pilgrimage trail through Staffordshire, that leads to the Shrine of St Chad near Lichfield Cathedral, passing through the woodlands of Cannock Chase(n)

programme.title: World's Sneakiest Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham features a host of animals that use deceptive tactics to find food. Some trick their prey, like the manipulative mantis which looks like a flower and killer whales which set traps using sound. Others cheat each other - sea otters demand ransoms after kidnapping each other's pups - and some, like African bird the drongo and monkeys, fool other species. In parks, meanwhile, grey squirrels are at war in stealing raids and it seems these animal liars, cheats and hustlers really will stop at nothing to get a meal(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: An incriminating gun troubles one team member, a link is revealed between Rory and Laser and Ash risks angering Bridget Kiely. Work draws Angela away from her family yet again(n)

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians take a trip on the Flying Scotsman, arguably the most famous locomotive in the world, along its original route connecting London and Edinburgh - the two most important financial capitals of the Empire. Peter Ginn explores how the railways revolutionised the delivery of mail across Britain, he and Alex Langlands learn about the first electronic communication service - more than 100 years before the arrival of email - and Ruth finds out about the role of the wheeltapper. Plus, why do some railway clocks have more than one minute hand?(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet UK
programme.desc: How doing the laundry is harming marine life, why the fish-and-chip supper has changed and which lobster is making a comeback. With Steve Brown, Gillian Burke and Chris Packham(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In this episode from Rochester Cathedral in Kent, Paul Martin meet the men of Chatham Dockyard, who kept the Royal Navy afloat and Elizabeth Talbot finds a rare Peter Rabbit book(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jamelia, Susan Calman, Gareth Malone and Paul Martin test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's tour reaches its final stop, Singapore, where he learns about the island city's maritime origins and the vision of the 19th-century Englishman who put it on the map. At the bar of the Raffles Hotel, he treats himself to a Singapore sling, now a world-famous cocktail, takes a boat down the river and dines with an artist and his family in Chinatown(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: TV presenting duo Sam Nixon and Mark Rhodes join experts Charles Hanson and Natasha Raskin Sharp on the hunt for bargains to sell at auction. Charles and Mark discover one of their items is not all it seems, while Sam and Natasha find a clock with dual nationality before the contest is settled at an auction in Darlington, Co Durham(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum
programme.desc: Victoria & Albert curators Steph and Jenny plan a major new exhibition celebrating the work of British fashion designer Mary Quant. A Yorkshire woman donates a 1960s minidress that would be perfect - but it needs a lot of restoration work to make it exhibition-ready. Curator Julius uncovers some of the first objects ever given to the museum, including a hand-painted writing case that once belonged to Queen Victoria, while Corinna hits the streets to gather the most significant designs of today - the flags and logo of climate change activists Extinction Rebellion(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: At Aintree Hospital's major trauma centre, a 15-strong team try to save the life of a young man who has arrived by emergency helicopter. He has been stabbed in the chest - one of three stabbings to arrive during the day. Across the city at the Royal Liverpool University Hospital, staff deal with a homeless man who has been returning for at least 18 years. He is always treated with patience and dignity and given somewhere to sleep his alcohol or substance misuse. However, his increasingly aggressive behaviour leads to him being issued with an ultimatum(n)

programme.title: Mum
programme.desc: It's Derek's birthday and the family come together to get the house ready for the party. Subtle family comedy, starring Lesley Manville, Peter Mullan, Ross Boatman and Dorothy Atkinson(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's main news events(n)

programme.title: Win the Wilderness: Alaska
programme.desc: Three of the couples are taken on a demanding and dangerous trek to Canwell Glacier system, an area that is home to wolves and bears. They face a gruelling journey over rough glacial terrain and icy streams, and soon find evidence that there are wild animals in the vicinity. As the competitors sleep under the stars, Duane and Rena have to decide who the next pair to be eliminated will be(n)

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: The largest boats in the fishing fleet head out to sea in autumn, including giant netters and trawlers up to 100 feet long capable of fishing hundreds of miles from shore. On one boat, an engineer and deckhand with 10 years of service behind him is training to become a captain, under the watchful eye of his veteran skipper. The vessel faces a rough week at sea in gale force winds. Back on land, the owner of a shellfish company that employs workers from Latvia and Lithuania fears for the future as Britain leaves the EU(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood gets a taste of life as a police officer in Aransas Pass on the Texas Gulf Coast, an area with a crime rate 90 per cent higher than the rest of the state(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Michael Mosley tests whether diet can affect mental health, and Alain Gregoire investigates the future technology that could spot signs of dementia before it develops. Elsewhere, Zoe Williams reveals a new treatment for the skin condition psoriasis, and Guddi Singh finds out the best way to calm a crying baby(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Catching up with Andrew and Laura Evans, who headed to Perth for a week in 2016, trying out the properties, jobs and lifestyle available should they choose to move there for good(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Anita Manning value items brought to Blackburn Cathedral, while Paul Martin learns about how mill workers in the town used to be woken by a human alarm clock(n)

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams investigate rumours that swallows are present in Sussex in January. They also look at why there's been a severe lack of snow in Scotland this winter, and share an incredible spectacle in the form of flocks of brent geese migrating from Northern Canada to the urban streets of Dublin(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Walks of Life
programme.desc: JB Gill journeys along a 12-mile stretch of the River Severn in Shropshire to discover the ways civilisations have lived and worshipped along its banks since the dawn of man. Starting in the shadow of Ironbridge, he finds out how the family that constructed the bridge and kick-started the Industrial Revolution were guided by their Quaker faith. After a spot of coracling on the water, he stops at the ruins of Buildwas Abbey and meets a modern-day monk to find out how their penitent lifestyles have changed since the structure was in its prime. Later, JB visits the ruins of the Roman city of Wroxeter, before lending a hand at a local vineyard(n)

programme.title: World's Sneakiest Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham features animals that utilise devious tactics to win a mate and raise a family, such as bower birds and fiddler crabs, who use optical illusions to impress a female, and male topi antelope, who fake danger to give them an edge. He also looks at family life, including the chicks that blackmail their parents, and the honeyguide bird that dupes another species into raising its young(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Rory risks arousing suspicion when tempted to tamper with evidence, Sharon seeks an ally in her quest for the truth, while Keith risks walking into a trap(n)

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians explore the life of the South Devon branch line before the Beeching cuts of the 1960s. Peter Ginn joins a footplate crew and receives coal-shovelling tips from a veteran steam engine driver of 63 years' experience, and with Alex he learns about one of Scotland's most lucrative exports. Ruth Goodman finds out about the dangers involved in working on the Victorian railways, and how those who lost limbs were catered for by a dedicated prosthetics workshop. She also discovers how the railways came to the rescue when a deadly disease wiped out almost the entire stock of London cattle(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet UK
programme.desc: A look at whether herring, the 'silver darlings of the sea' are making a comeback. Plus, a man shares his passion for sea horses and Gillian Burke investigates whether being a brave crab is a disastrous strategy(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Philip Serrell hunt for objects to take to auction at Blackburn Cathedral, Lancashire. Charles values a wooden Fijian club, while two vintage enamel signs catch Philip's eye. Presenter Paul Martin takes a look at the impressive Talbot collection - local press photographs taken of events, celebrities and the royal family over a 50-year period(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jamelia, Susan Calman, Gareth Malone and Paul Martin test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the week's overall champion set to be crowned(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in the island city-state of Singapore on the last leg of his 2,500-mile exploration of south-east Asia. In Tai Seng he marvels at the world's largest subterranean rail depot where self-driving MRT trains are operated. He then takes a cable car to the island of Sentosa where he learns about one of Britain's worst military defeats and the brutal Japanese occupation that followed. At the Singapore Stock Exchange, Michael appreciates the island's success as a financial centre and, in the residential district of Ang Mo Kio, visits an urban farm of the future - on a car park roof(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the unpaid volunteers who sacrifice their free time to staff more than two hundred RNLI lifeboat stations across the UK. Matters they tend to on an ongoing basis include injured rock climbers and crashed paragliders(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedian Bobby Davro and songstress Sheila Ferguson of the Three Degrees compete to find profit-making antiques in Kent, guided by experts James Braxton and Catherine Southon. Away from the contest, Bobby hears the little-known history of the Royal Engineers, while Sheila discovers how black culture and music arrived - and thrived - in the Garden of England(n)

programme.title: Our Coast
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles and Mehreen Baig explore the Co Down coastline between Belfast and Strangford Lough, along the Ards Peninsula. In Belfast, Adrian road-tests the largest hydraulic crane in the world and helps park a 100-metre-long Dutch cargo ship. Mehreen goes kayaking on Strangford Lough to see how the currents of the UK's largest sea inlet is placing the area at the forefront of renewable energy research. Plus, Helen Skelton tests the waters of the North Channel, one of the most dangerous swims in the world, and local comedian Shane Todd visits a long-established bespoke racing-car factory(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: The first of two compilations of highlights from the 17th series of the quiz with a difference, hosted by Sandi Toksvig. Guests including Tom Allen, Jo Brand, Susan Calman, Alan Carr, Jimmy Carr, Phill Jupitus, Nish Kumar, Prue Leith, Joe Lycett, Jason Manford, Sarah Millican, David Mitchell, Johnny Vegas, Holly Walsh and Josh Widdicombe joined regular panellist Alan Davies to answer questions based on subjects beginning with the letter Q(n)

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's Tour of Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian explores Glasgow, calling in at a martial arts club in the east end and exploring the ruins of Crookston Castle. Frankie's home city is no stranger to riots and protest and he meets one high-profile campaigner who became famous as one of the 'Glasgow Girls'. On the journey to his stand-up show at the Gaiety Theatre in Ayr, Frankie pops into the town of Troon to meet some members of one of the most famous showmen families in Scotland. Upon reaching Ayr, he catches up with fellow performer Elaine C Smith to discuss performing onstage and their different styles of comedy(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary Beard and her panel of guests explore the links between crime and culture. Together, they pick over why the detective novel is such a big part of the literary and theatrical landscape, as well as the wider responsibilities of writers who produce crime narratives. Moreover, they debate whether the cultural representation of crime is sometimes to blame for encouraging violence in real life(n)

programme.title: In the Heat of the Night (1967)
programme.desc: When a wealthy industrialist is murdered in Mississippi, the bigoted local sheriff is quick to accuse a black newcomer to town - only to learn his suspect is a highly respected homicide detective from Philadelphia. However, the duo are forced to put aside their mutual loathing and team up to track down the killer. Oscar-winning drama, starring Sidney Poitier, Rod Steiger and Warren Oates(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: Dr Adam Rutherford tells the story of how Dr Henry Gray and Dr Henry Carter produced academic textbook Gray's Anatomy - the world's most famous study of the human body. Based on dissections they performed themselves, the accuracy of its descriptions and the stark beauty of its illustrations made it an instant bestseller(n)

programme.title: World's Busiest Railway 2015
programme.desc: The team considers the challenges faced by Mumbai's Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus and ask what the future holds for the city's 5.5million rail commuters. Anita Rani tackles the railway's head of safety on the measures they're taking to reduce the fatality rate of nine people a day, while Dan Snow meets two eyewitnesses to 2008's terror attacks that claimed the lives of 52 people at the station. John Sergeant reveals the heavy human cost of building the line that connects Mumbai to the south and east of India, and Robert Llewellyn fulfills a childhood dream and becomes a train driver for a day(n)

programme.title: A Very British History
programme.desc: Vlogger Shu Lin explores the history of the Chinese community in the UK from the 1950s through to the present, day, revealing how these migrants transformed Britain's bland post-war dining scene. She finds out how an area of Soho became today's vibrant Chinatown and traces how the community has transformed from relying heavily on the restaurant trade, to one achieving outstanding business and educational success(n)

programme.title: The Ruth Ellis Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Gillian Pachter continues her forensic look at the Ruth Ellis case, this time focusing on revelations regarding the other man in Ruth's life and the role he played in the murder(n)

programme.title: EastEnders: Back to Ours
programme.desc: Barbara Windsor and Pam St Clement share some afternoon tea and reflect on memorable Peggy and Pat moments from the soap(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: There are fireworks of a different kind for Pat, Roy, Peggy and Frank as Albert Square celebrates Bonfire Night. An edition of the soap from November 2000(n)

programme.title: Art, Passion & Power: The Story of the Royal Collection
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores how royal collecting has changed since the days of Queen Victoria. He also reveals how women, both queens and consorts, made their mark on the Collection, and used art to steady the ship of monarchy through an uncertain age(n)

programme.title: Victorian Sensations
programme.desc: Actor Paul McGann explores the 1890s, a decade when science, entertainment, art and morality collided. He discovers how the works of HG Wells, Aubrey Beardsley and Oscar Wilde were shaped by fears of moral, social and racial degeneration, and explains how Wilde sought to subvert traditional Victorian values(n)

programme.title: What Do Artists Do All Day?
programme.desc: Internationally renowned abstract artist Frank Bowling became the first black Royal Academician in 2005. As a major retrospective opens at Tate Britain, Brenda Emmanus meets him to look back over his long career and there are interviews with critics and fellow artists to discuss the significance of his work in the history of British art(n)

programme.title: A Very British History
programme.desc: Vlogger Shu Lin explores the history of the Chinese community in the UK from the 1950s through to the present, day, revealing how these migrants transformed Britain's bland post-war dining scene. She finds out how an area of Soho became today's vibrant Chinatown and traces how the community has transformed from relying heavily on the restaurant trade, to one achieving outstanding business and educational success(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: The Pennine Way
programme.desc: Polar adventurer Paul Rose explores rural Northumberland as he embarks on the last stage of his journey along the Pennine Way. He makes a remarkable discovery at the Roman archaeological dig at Vindolanda, finds out what lies beyond the red flags while on exercise with the Grenadier Guards and discovers how night-time walkers can have an almost magical experience in an area acclaimed for the quality of its dark skies(n)

programme.title: Walt Disney
programme.desc: Conclusion of the documentary exploring the life, career and legacy of Walt Disney, making use of archive footage alongside interviews with his friends, colleagues and relatives to present a portrait of a ruthlessly driven animator and entrepreneur whose single-minded ambition allowed him to create a string of cartoon masterpieces that would define an entire industry. The second episode explores Disney's later years, as he made films such as Cinderella and Mary Poppins, and realised his dream project - Disneyland(n)

programme.title: Inside Chernobyl's Mega Tomb
programme.desc: Documentary following the construction of a 36,000 tonne steel structure to entomb the ruins of the nuclear power plant destroyed in the 1986 disaster. A team of international engineers race to build the new steel structure, but radiation levels make it almost impossible for workers to build right on top of the old reactor. The team face difficult challenges as they attempt to erect this new steel mega-structure and move it into place(n)

programme.title: The Ruth Ellis Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Gillian Pachter focuses on the three weeks leading up to Ruth Ellis's execution, and the last-minute attempts to save her life even though Ruth herself was determined to die. The killer finally admitted she had an accomplice, prompting her solicitor to race to the Home Office in a bid to stop the execution. But it was all in vain and Ruth was hanged. Gillian goes on to explore the role of her case in the introduction of the defence of diminished responsibility in England and its place in the eventual abolition of capital punishment in 1965(n)

programme.title: How We Built Britain
programme.desc: David Dimbleby travels west to discover the impact of Georgian architectural theory, exploring the Stourhead garden in Wiltshire and taking in the grandeur of Oxfordshire's Blenheim Palace. He travels by sedan chair through Bath, taking the waters and extolling the virtues of the terraced house, visits the tin mines of Cornwall and crosses the Menai Bridge into Anglesey before ending his journey in Dublin(n)

programme.title: Don McCullin: Looking for England
programme.desc: Following the 83-year-old photographer as he documents England, from inner cities to seaside towns, on a journey in search of his own nation. Sixty years after starting out, McCullin returns to his old haunts in the East End of London, Bradford, Consett, Eastbourne and Scarborough, encountering an array of characters along the way(n)

programme.title: The Silk Road
programme.desc: Sam Willis travels through Central Asia. In Tajikistan, he meets the last survivors of the Sogdian people, a culture that proved vital to the success of the Silk Road and traded from the China Sea to the Mediterranean. He also visits the Uzbek cities of Samarkand and Bukhara, discovering how the conqueror Timur captured large numbers of craftsmen to build them, and discovers how trade between nations led to the birth of astronomy and modern mathematics(n)

programme.title: Mars Uncovered: Ancient God of War
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes investigates the enduring relationship between warfare and worship by following the trail of the ancient Roman god of war Mars. She begins in Carthage, site of one of the most significant and bloodiest victories in the history of the Roman Empire and explores the vital role of Mars in Rome's Imperial expansion. Bettany also considers how the figure of Mars was used by artists such as Botticelli and Rubens to examine the inevitability of war, and whether the benefits of war still outweigh the horrors(n)

programme.title: Inside Chernobyl's Mega Tomb
programme.desc: Documentary following the construction of a 36,000 tonne steel structure to entomb the ruins of the nuclear power plant destroyed in the 1986 disaster. A team of international engineers race to build the new steel structure, but radiation levels make it almost impossible for workers to build right on top of the old reactor. The team face difficult challenges as they attempt to erect this new steel mega-structure and move it into place(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Bruno Brookes host the edition first shown on March 30, with performances by Pat and Mick, the Bangles, Roachford, Brother Beyond, Paul Simpson featuring Adeva, Transvision Vamp, the The, Guns N' Roses, Fuzzbox, the Cult, Madonna and Kon Kan(n)

programme.title: Sings Disney Songs
programme.desc: Interpretations of music from the Walt Disney Studios' films over the past 40 years, featuring songs including the Bare Necessities, When You Wish Upon a Star and Zip-a-Dee-Doo-Dah. Celine Dion and Elton John are among the artists on clips from shows including Top of the Pops, Blue Peter, Shirley Bassey, It's Lulu, Louis Armstrong's Show of the Week and Brubeck(n)

programme.title: Rock Island Line: The Song That Made Britain Rock
programme.desc: Billy Bragg offers a salute to American folk song Rock Island Line, which emerged in the late 1920s. Its first recorded performance of was by inmates of the Arkansas Cummins State Farm prison in 1934. After several covers, Lonnie Donegan's recording, released as a single in late 1955, signalled the start of the UK skiffle craze(n)

programme.title: Chas and Dave: Last Orders
programme.desc: Documentary offering an insight into Chas Hodges and Dave Peacock's successful five decades in the music industry, which has seen them share the same bill as Led Zeppelin and make a big impact on London's cultural landscape. The film blends archive performances with more recent tour footage and features contributions by Pete Doherty, Jools Holland and Phill Jupitus. Narrated by Arthur Smith(n)

programme.title: What Ever Happened to Rock 'n' Roll?
programme.desc: Lauren Laverne leads a discussion from London's 100 Club asking whether rock 'n' roll is in crisis and what it represents in contemporary society. Can the genre still be considered as dangerous and subversive as it once was or has it become more about lifestyle and decoration? Participants include Savages' lead singer Jehnny Beth, Dr John Cooper Clarke and former Animal Eric Burdon. There are also contributions from Noel Gallagher and Dave Grohl and the members of Sleaford Mods and Alabama Shakes(n)

Sharon tells Dennis they are leaving Walford for good but her plans are thwarted when she bumps into a raging Ben - and that's only the start of her problems(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman presents the final, which sees the last three contestants rising to the challenge. To shake things up, however, the judges present their tasks in a different order to normal. Chris Bavin's 'rustle up' comes first, after which it's on to another of Angela Hartnett's tricky recipes in the eliminator. Sadly, one of the hopefuls will then receive their marching orders, leaving the other two to take on Mary Berry's challenge - a full three-course meal which will secure one of them the title(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The owner of a hair salon is killed in front of her family, leaving DI Neville Parker and his team stumped when it turns out no one actually saw what happened. Ralf Little stars as the British detective, with Aude Legastelois-Bidé, Tobi Bakare and Shyko Amos(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Weymouth, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: A Cut Too Far? Male Circumcision
programme.desc: An investigation into the rights and wrongs of male circumcision, one of the most common surgical procedures in the world. In the UK, thousands of baby boys are circumcised every year for religious or cultural reasons. But it is becoming increasingly controversial. Journalist Adnan Sarwar, who was circumcised by his Muslim parents as an infant, presents this investigation into the operation and its safety, finding out whether it is an outmoded practice or a harmless expression of cultural and religious freedoms(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Jon Kay and Rachel Burden(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: The penguin keepers embark on their most ambitious project to date, building an underwater feeding machine, while a hyrax is rushed to the vet for some emergency dental work. Jean Johansson discovers how wolves like to bury their food so they can come back to eat it when they are hungry. Ben Fogle and Kate Humble meet a new creature at the park - and it is the male, not the female that gives birth to their young(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the development progress of properties in Canterbury in Kent, London's Crystal Palace and the Cumbria village of Branthwaite(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Jayne Myburgh thought her son Albie's rare genetic disorder would be greater controlled in the warmth of the Australian sun. Her South African-born husband Eugene was well aware of his wife's dreams, but needed more than a promise of sunshine to convince him he should move to the other side of the world. Nicki Chapman finds out whether or not they made the life-changing decision to relocate Down Under(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK

programme.desc: Three influential farm workers - all critical to the success of their farms - go head to head, hoping to be named Farm Worker of the Year at the prestigious Farmers Weekly Awards(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Margie Cooper and Charles Hanson reach the final leg of their trip and steer their classic car through Yorkshire. Charles decides to spend as much of his kitty as possible, hoping a bigger outlay will result in huge profits. He goes for age, finding a pair of expensive 17th-century oak chairs. Margie also goes 17th century, but finds a crib that's much more within budget. The final auction is in Harrogate, North Yorkshire(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Six Nations Sin Bin

Plus, Wales, England, Ireland and Scotland battle it out to see who can be crowned the Sin Bin Showdown champion(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Host Tudur Owen discovers the truth about family life, referencing the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television. It's a story of disastrous holidays, arranged marriages and wonky houses, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Crystal Palace left-back Patrick van Aanholt, former Premier League star Jimmy Floyd Hasselbaink, England cricketer Samit Patel and tennis player Naomi Broady join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chaos and panic ensues as the party goers struggle to comprehend what has happened. As the grave situation grows worse, multiple lives are left hanging in the balance and one person is set to meet a tragic end(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for another episode of previously unseen material from the hit comedy panel show. Among the participants setting out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves are Clare Balding, Liz Bonnin, Jo Brand, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Greg Davies, Simon Day, Greg James, Guz Khan, Gabby Logan, Chris McCausland, Stephen Merchant, Kiri Pritchard-McLean, Angela Scanlon, John Simm, Ranj Singh, Henning Wehn, Claudia Winkleman and Lucy Worsley(n)

programme.title: Bumps
programme.desc: Sixty-two-year-old divorcee Anita is concerned that despite having two grown up kids, there is no stork on the horizon to bring her grandchildren. Her daughter Joanne cannot have children and her son Aiden has zero interest. After hearing about an Indian woman in her seventies giving birth, Anita secretly undergoes treatment - only to learn Joanne has also managed to conceive and is due at the same time. One-off comedy, starring Amanda Redman, with Lisa McGrillis and Seb Cardinal(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham brings the 26th series of his hit chat show to an end by dipping back into its archives to recall some of the more memorable verbal exchanges. programme.title: Age of Outrage
programme.desc: Comedy sketches for the internet age, featuring the work of new Welsh writing talent. With Miriam Isaac, Geraint Rhys 'Edwards, Zak Ghazi-Torbati and Mali Tudno-Jones. Plus, a special appearance by 'godfather of gunge' Dave Benson-Phillips(n)

Among the participants setting out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves are Clare Balding, Liz Bonnin, Jo Brand, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Greg Davies, Simon Day, Greg James, Guz Khan, Gabby Logan, Chris McCausland, Stephen Merchant, Kiri Pritchard-McLean, Angela Scanlon, John Simm, Ranj Singh, Henning Wehn, Claudia Winkleman and Lucy Worsley(n)

programme.title: Bumps
programme.desc: Sixty-two-year-old divorcee Anita is concerned that despite having two grown up kids, there is no stork on the horizon to bring her grandchildren. Her daughter Joanne cannot have children and her son Aiden has zero interest. After hearing about an Indian woman in her seventies giving birth, Anita secretly undergoes treatment - only to learn Joanne has also managed to conceive and is due at the same time. One-off comedy, starring Amanda Redman, with Lisa McGrillis and Seb Cardinal(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham brings the 26th series of his hit chat show to an end by dipping back into its archives to recall some of the more memorable verbal exchanges. programme.desc: The tension between Al and Daniel continues to build. Meanwhile, Emma tries to calm an angry patient who is nursing old wounds(n)

programme.desc: Frank Hathaway and Luella Shakespeare investigate the disappearance of an Eastern European oligarch. Comedy drama starring Mark Benton and Jo Joyner(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Hampshire with a dynamic couple who love the outdoors and have £650,000 to buy a property and, while in the county, visits the Solent Sky Museum(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Northern Ireland's first charcuterie maker, Alistair, is joined by Kenny and Jennifer, as they aim to sell their chorizo, sausages and meat at the Irish Game Fair(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Margie Cooper and Charles Hanson are in Northumberland and Tyne and Wear, where Margie finds a brass antique but she is baffled as to what it could be, and in a pub in Gateshead, Charles is advised how best to use a sword when dancing(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Matt Baker(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team helps project manager Ben in Avening in the Cotswolds, whose life was changed in a split second by a freak accident while holidaying in Cyprus. Ben slipped and fell head first into the shallow end of a pool, breaking his neck in three places, and for him to move back home from Salisbury Spinal Unit, it needs to be adapted with a front access and a two-storey extension with through-floor lift, along with all the bespoke care provision that he will need, including a home-office space(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, the spy cameras travel to the Arctic and Antarctic circles, following chicks in a king penguin colony on the island of South Georgia in the Southern Ocean. On the other side of the world, Spy Walrus discovers a walrus family hauled out on an ice floe, before Spy Gyrfalcon follows the family as they head towards dry land. Elsewhere on Norway's Hornoya island, Spy Puffin follows thousands of male puffins as they gather in the snow and search for their life-long partners(n)

programme.title: Nolan Live
programme.desc: Stephen Nolan probes the issues that matter to the people of Northern Ireland, focusing on the stories of the week and the people making the headlines in lively debates with the studio audience and guest panellists(n)

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Freddie has unanswered questions for his ex, Zak, and hopes that Zak can be honest about why they split. Holly tries to work out why Ben dumped her after she met his family(n)

programme.title: My Mate's a Bad Date
programme.desc: Oloni has his work cut out with Vicky as he helps her find her perfect match, hoping to change her behaviour and her unrealistic criteria for her suitors(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Catching up with Andrew and Laura Evans, who headed to Perth for a week in 2016, trying out the properties, jobs and lifestyle available should they choose to move there for good(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers investigate a factory fire that forces residents to evacuate their homes, and a tipper truck is caught on camera as it illegally dumps 20 tons of waste(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Mother and daughter Bethan and Elinor compete against Philip, Emma and their family, presenting their sheep at the Royal Welsh Show, the key event in the agricultural calendar(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Margie Cooper and Charles Hanson visit the North Yorkshire town of Harrogate, where Margie tucks into some rhubarb crumble and hopes a large album of Second World War maps will achieve her victory at auction, while Charles risks a £200 deal on the toss of a coin(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Alex Jones present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben goes to extreme lengths to stop Keanu from killing Callum, while Phil sets out on his own revenge mission against Keanu - leading both Mitchells to the boat on the Thames for the ultimate showdown. Sharon tells Dennis they are leaving Walford for good but her plans are thwarted when she bumps into a raging Ben - and that's only the start of her problems(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman presents the final, which sees the last three contestants rising to the challenge. To shake things up, however, the judges present their tasks in a different order to normal. Chris Bavin's 'rustle up' comes first, after which it's on to another of Angela Hartnett's tricky recipes in the eliminator. Sadly, one of the hopefuls will then receive their marching orders, leaving the other two to take on Mary Berry's challenge - a full three-course meal which will secure one of them the title(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The owner of a hair salon is killed in front of her family, leaving DI Neville Parker and his team stumped when it turns out no one actually saw what happened. Ralf Little stars as the British detective, with Aude Legastelois-Bidé, Tobi Bakare and Shyko Amos(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Weymouth, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: A Cut Too Far? Male Circumcision
programme.desc: An investigation into the rights and wrongs of male circumcision, one of the most common surgical procedures in the world. In the UK, thousands of baby boys are circumcised every year for religious or cultural reasons. But it is becoming increasingly controversial. Journalist Adnan Sarwar, who was circumcised by his Muslim parents as an infant, presents this investigation into the operation and its safety, finding out whether it is an outmoded practice or a harmless expression of cultural and religious freedoms(n)

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Jon Kay and Rachel Burden(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: The penguin keepers embark on their most ambitious project to date, building an underwater feeding machine, while a hyrax is rushed to the vet for some emergency dental work. Jean Johansson discovers how wolves like to bury their food so they can come back to eat it when they are hungry. Ben Fogle and Kate Humble meet a new creature at the park - and it is the male, not the female that gives birth to their young(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Jayne Myburgh thought her son Albie's rare genetic disorder would be greater controlled in the warmth of the Australian sun. Her South African-born husband Eugene was well aware of his wife's dreams, but needed more than a promise of sunshine to convince him he should move to the other side of the world. Nicki Chapman finds out whether or not they made the life-changing decision to relocate Down Under(n)

programme.title: Save Money: Lose Weight
programme.desc: Over the past 25 years, portion sizes have increased by 100 per cent - and plates have got bigger too - none of which is good news for people's diet. With this in mind, Ranj Singh checks out the breakfast buffet to see whether people know how many calories they are piling on their plates and Sian Williams tests off-the-shelf portion-control gadgets, including smaller cutlery to help eat more slowly. Plus, two more diets are put to the test - the Metabolism Reset Diet, based around a detox for the liver, and Bosh! Healthy Vegan, which means ditching the junk(n)

programme.title: Tyson Fury: The Gypsy King
programme.desc: The heavyweight celebrates a victory over Thomas Schwartz in Las Vegas by enjoying a night of heavy drinking back home in Morecambe and video-calling rival Anthony Joshua. This angers his wife Paris, who is reminded of his alcohol abuse during his years of depression, and Fury looks to make amends by treating the family to a day out in Blackpool and booking a family holiday. The boxer then heads to Marbella with trainer Ben Davison, before preparing to face his next opponent Otto Wallin back in Vegas, in what proves to be a challenging fight(n)

Even more sinister is the picture that starts to emerge as they match the nuns to a series of unsolved missing persons cases from the previous five years. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Save Money: Lose Weight
programme.desc: Over the past 25 years, portion sizes have increased by 100 per cent - and plates have got bigger too - none of which is good news for people's diet. With this in mind, Ranj Singh checks out the breakfast buffet to see whether people know how many calories they are piling on their plates and Sian Williams tests off-the-shelf portion-control gadgets, including smaller cutlery to help eat more slowly. Plus, two more diets are put to the test - the Metabolism Reset Diet, based around a detox for the liver, and Bosh! Healthy Vegan, which means ditching the junk(n)

programme.title: Tyson Fury: The Gypsy King
programme.desc: The heavyweight celebrates a victory over Thomas Schwartz in Las Vegas by enjoying a night of heavy drinking back home in Morecambe and video-calling rival Anthony Joshua. This angers his wife Paris, who is reminded of his alcohol abuse during his years of depression, and Fury looks to make amends by treating the family to a day out in Blackpool and booking a family holiday. The boxer then heads to Marbella with trainer Ben Davison, before preparing to face his next opponent Otto Wallin back in Vegas, in what proves to be a challenging fight(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: When a woman is found dead laid out in the shape of a crucifix on Jesus Green, Will and Geordie are led to a nearby convent where they meet steely Sister Grace, who rules over her flock with an iron fist. Even more sinister is the picture that starts to emerge as they match the nuns to a series of unsolved missing persons cases from the previous five years. Meanwhile, when Leonard's father, Russell, arrives in Grantchester, it's clear there's no love lost between parent and son. Robson Green, Tom Brittney, Tracy-Ann Oberman and Sean Gilder star(n)

programme.title: Cold Feet
programme.desc: Adam and Karen's future is thrown into jeopardy by an unexpected offer, and David is in turmoil over his growing feelings for Robyn. While Jenny braces herself for her first mammogram after her treatment for cancer, Pete helps their son Adam realise his dream. programme.desc: Oxford's last-surviving all-female college is holding a reunion to bid farewell to one of its most prominent professors. However, the evening leads to tragedy when a guest is found murdered, and Lewis is convinced the killing is connected to an attack that occurred at the same institution 10 years previously. The detective calls in his former DS, Alison McLennan, to help with the investigation, but the case takes a twist when two more bodies turn up. Crime drama, guest starring Juliet Stevenson and Saskia Reeves, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Wild Bill

Bill and Muriel find themselves on opposite sides and both are determined to prove themselves correct. Meanwhile, Bill wrestles with introducing Mary to Kelsey, Kelsey makes a new friend at the local comprehensive, and Lydia resents doing Bill's dirty work as the redundancies commence(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective gains a taste of prison life while investigating the murder of an inmate jailed for a property swindle. The victim's two partners-in-crime are also incarcerated in the same low-security cell block. programme.title: Midsomer Murders

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa has problems at school with Toby, Angela Sim's nephew. Meanwhile, Al has a 'Large Whiskey' order and has to ask Bert for some help, and Mrs Tishell seems to be having a delayed onset of mourning. Elsewhere, Martin and Louisa decide to try Mel's suggestions of using amber beads to help James while he is teething(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Penhale decides to have a Police Open day to raise community awareness, but the only genuine visitor is an angry farmer who has had three of her sheep stolen. Meanwhile, Morwenna decides to take Al away for the weekend to try out Hannah's new glamping site, but the trip does not go well as Al accidently burns down the yurt. Elsewhere, James has started biting not only Louisa but other children at the nursery. Louisa speaks to Tina about it, but is surprised to find out Tina has spoken to their lecturer about it too(n)

programme.title: Wild Bill
programme.desc: Suspicion falls on members of the immigrant workforce after a suitcase containing the body of farm owner Darren Bailey is pulled from the river. Bill discovers that the farm had been used to launder criminal proceeds through the clever accounting of the victim's much younger Slovenian girlfriend, Lubica, who has been made sole owner of the farm's assets. This makes her a key suspect, particularly after it's revealed that her and Darren had an argument on the night he went missing. However, with many of Darren's employees seeming happy at the news of his demise, and stories coming to light about how he squeezed them at every opportunity, it isn't an open and shut case. Starring Rob Lowe, Rachael Stirling and Angela Griffin(n)

programme.title: Wild Bill
programme.desc: The discovery of a pyre of bodies puts Bill on the hunt for Frank McGill, a hitman notorious for his weapon of choice - a nail-gun. However, having been put into witness protection years earlier after turning in other members of the Maguire gang he worked for, Frank is now off-grid, so Bill sets his sights on tracking him down. Meanwhile, DS Blair is released on bail after information is leaked regarding the unlawful obtaining of key DNA, Commissioner Metcalfe discusses the financial benefits of selling off a disused training facility and the pressure mounts for Bill to resign. Starring Rob Lowe and Rachael Stirling(n)

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Samir is promised a better-paid job at the Casablanca, and Deirdre is stunned when he proposes to her. Des offers Curly employment at the betting shop(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe's boss is concerned about her reputation affecting the business. Eric offers to help Alan with his debts, but says he will require the Woolpack as security(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Peggy begins a frantic search for David's dog Deefer after a pair of youths steal her car from outside the Aidensfield Arms with the hound in the boot. programme.desc: Part two of two. Preparations get under way for the christening, while Primrose loses her heart to a very confused vicar. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A barrister is killed with a lethal dose of morphine and suspicion falls on ex-convict Grady Felton, who has recently returned to the village where he committed murder years earlier. However, he has a strong alibi and Barnaby thinks he must be working with an accomplice - until Grady himself is targeted in an arson attack. Detective drama, guest starring Sharon Duce, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Wild Bill
programme.desc: Bill launches a PR offensive when a leaked list of potential redundancies turns his team against him. Meanwhile, a routine burglary turns out to be a much bigger case than first anticipated, and Bill finds himself facing a public panic. DNA discovered at the scene of the crime provides the team with a match, but with the culprit missing, they track down his estranged daughter in the search for answers. Elsewhere, Bill's relationship with Mary begins to deepen, and Oleg keeps up the pressure on Muriel(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle's country holiday with their latest girlfriends spirals into chaos when they are held hostage in a farmhouse by two gunmen who have just botched a robbery. Adventure, with Gordon Jackson, Lewis Collins and Martin Shaw(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan uses a criminal's abandoned dog as bait to lure his remorseful owner back into the country from France. Alfred Marks, Brian Glover and Brian Wilde guest star, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The coroner sets out to improve his bedside manner by spending time with a doctor, learning a valuable lesson in humility from a mother dying of a terminal illness(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A Theo discovers his teenage godson has turned to crime, and tries to set him straight - but a gangster hopes to recruit him for a diamond heist. F Murray Abraham guest stars(n)

programme.title: The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A priceless French diamond is stolen from its London display. However, with Holmes missing, Watson and Mycroft are forced to pursue the investigation themselves. Detective drama, guest starring Charles Gray, with Jeremy Brett and Edward Hardwicke(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is asked for protection by newsagent Mr Mukerjee, who has been sent death threats and had a brick thrown through his window. When Terry goes along, he discovers that the problem may be that the shopowner has promised his daughter's hand in marriage to more than one man. Guest starring Saeed Jaffrey(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie's sanity is questioned when he becomes preoccupied with a gang of Hell's Angels and puts his life in jeopardy by taking part in a motorbike race to the death. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan persuades a villain to act as an informant providing evidence against his two accomplices. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman, and featuring an early screen appearance by Christopher Ellison(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Westlife's Nicky Byrne, Olympian Amy Williams, Natalie Cassidy (EastEnders' Sonia Jackson) and Dave Myers of The Hairy Bikers pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly

programme.title: The Professionals

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Recently released criminal Eddie Boyse becomes prime suspect in an assault case - and Regan believes he is guilty. programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four celebrity contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Emmerdale's Michael Parr (Ross Barton), England footballer Alex Scott, Hollyoaks actress Jennifer Metcalfe (Mercedes McQueen) and TV presenter Bill Turnbull must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Passenger experience manager Demi deals with some disgruntled passengers, while check-in attendants Carrie-Anne and Selina tackle broken baggage belts. Elsewhere, Border force worker Bob has to decide whether he should let a young Brazilian student into the UK to visit his brother(n)

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: The documentary going behind the scenes of the London airport returns for a fifth season. Inside the terminal, seasoned pro Demi dishes out deals for delayed passengers, while outside on the airfield, Ian is on the runway as it goes into lockdown with a full armed police presence. For the first time this series, viewers will also see the work of the Border Force's canine drugs detection team - Rachel and her dog Cole, who in his career has intercepted more than £1million worth of class-A contraband. Narrated by Julie Walters(n)

programme.title: The Outlaw Josey Wales (1976)
programme.desc: A Confederate soldier refuses to surrender at the end of the Civil War, and is branded a dangerous outlaw. Crossing America in search of sanctuary from the army and bounty hunters, he only wants solitude - but ends up with a strange assortment of travelling companions. Western, directed by and starring Clint Eastwood. With Chief Dan George and Sondra Locke(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar is asked by the former king of Fedyra to accompany him on an important mission in Switzerland, where he becomes aide to a queen(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is captivated by an attractive female vocalist, who boasts limited musical talent. Nevertheless, he uses his contacts to secure a gig for her at a club run by a dodgy entertainment manager, and soon both he and Terry find themselves immersed in the seedier side of the showbiz industry during a night's work in a disco. Comedy drama, starring Dennis Waterman and George Cole(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 investigates the fatal shooting of a South American president's son at a polo match, and finds that the victim's step-sister is unknowingly dating one of the terrorists involved. Ruby Wax and Pierce Brosnan feature in early screen appearances, alongside stars Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Gordon Jackson(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan is forced to impose himself on an old girlfriend whose flat is conveniently near a bank that intelligence has revealed is to be raided. Once in position, he and Carter try to pinpoint a radio transmitter that is being used to coordinate the movements of the robbers. Detective drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh returns with the celebrity version of the team quiz show. As ever, four star contestants are challenged to pit their wits against one of the show's ruthless quiz experts, in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for their charity of choice. This time, Absolute Radio's Christian O'Connell, singer, TV presenter and vocal coach Carrie Grant, Channel 5 children's presenter Naomi Wilkinson and I, Daniel Blake star Dave Johns each try to race down the game board without being caught, before joining forces for the final chase(n)

programme.title: From Russia with Love (1963)
programme.desc: James Bond is sent to steal a top-secret Soviet decoding machine and ends up helping its operator defect to the West. However, he discovers the mission is really a trap set by international crime syndicate Spectre, and struggles to evade his would-be assassins. Spy thriller, starring Sean Connery, Robert Shaw, Daniela Bianchi and Lotte Lenya(n)

programme.title: Tony Robinson: Coast to Coast
programme.desc: The presenter visits Eden Valley, Cumbria, where he makes a dramatic ascent to the limestone-paved Great Asby Scar, the last evidence of a 300 million-year-old tropical sea. Smardale Gill viaduct has Tony marvelling at its engineering genius, before he investigates the 21st-century technology driving a traditional dairy. The actor and history buff gets a masterclass working with border collie sheepdogs before heading to Nine Standards Rigg, a summit high on the backbone of Britain, where he contemplates the origins of the huge drystone cairns that dominate the skyline(n)

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Expats Graham and Linda hope want to open a takeaway in Benalmadena which specialises in selling pies, but their dream has been put on hold until the council grants them permission. Will it all prove to be pie in the sky? Caravan site boss Bambi and her neighbour Johnny, who is also her former dance partner, try to rekindle their old magic at the campsite charity fete. Taffy and Leanne have set up their own pool maintenance business - but it's their own pool that is causing them the biggest headache(n)

programme.title: Hoarders
programme.desc: Decluttering experts Caz and Tee set out to help Plymouth pensioner Arthur, whose two-bedroom flat is packed with piles of junk he cannot bear to throw away. A carpet of newspapers covers every inch of the floor and as a result, family and friends have stopped visiting. This might just be the duo's toughest assignment yet. Meanwhile, Khim Sim is getting married to her fiancé Jim, but first she must clear her hoard so he can move in, and in London, Mandi's passion for vintage clothes is out of control(n)

programme.title: Addicted to Food: Breaking the Cycle
programme.desc: Therapist Mandy Saligari, who specialises in addiction, visits a woman for five intensive therapy sessions, to get to the root of her addiction to food. Kara weighs 24 stone and and as a single mother of two, she knows there is a lot at stake. But the trauma of her father's death and her marriage breakdown set her on a destructive path. Now she admits to binges and struggles to say no to treats. Can Mandy help her break this vicious circle before it's too late?(n)

programme.title: Curvy Girls: Stripped Bare
programme.desc: First in a two-part documentary going behind the scenes of leading London fashion agency Milk, which specialises in supplying plus-size models. Owner Anna Shillinglaw set up the agency with a handful of girls seven years ago but now has more than 100 'curvy' models on her books. Here, Felicity, Sonny and Hayley reveal how they were discovered, talk about the impact modelling has had on their lives and how they deal with the backlash from those who claim they are promoting obesity(n)

programme.title: Curvy Girls: Stripped Bare
programme.desc: Second of a two-part documentary going behind the scenes of London fashion agency Milk, which specialises in plus-size models. Three women reveal the highs and lows of the work, and what it is like selling clothes for a growing legion of household fashion brands. Lauren's career is going from strength to strength since she joined Milk and there is more good news on the horizon, while new girl Charlotte reveals how she is known as an 'inbetweenie' who sits below the bigger sizes(n)
